title: euphrasia caudata
intro: Euphrasia caudata, also known as the long-stalked eyebright, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and elongated stalks. Traditionally, Euphrasia caudata has been used in herbal medicine to treat eye and vision problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to provide relief from conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and other eye conditions, as well as reduce eye strain and improve vision clarity. Euphrasia caudata can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or eye drops, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
